hypothesis: The bug is in the validators - they might not be catching the corrupted data
action: Reviewed validators.py and added more validation checks
result: Validation still passes even with corrupted data because validation checks happen after mutations

hypothesis: The rollback mechanism in pipeline.py is not working correctly
action: Added debug output to track rollback stack and data state
result: Rollback stack is populated but the original_data was already mutated before the exception occurred

hypothesis: The transforms are mutating data in-place instead of returning copies
action: Traced through transform functions and noticed data['name'] is modified directly
result: Identified root cause: transforms.py modifies input dict in-place. When pipeline.py does copy.deepcopy(original_data), the copy contains references to the same nested objects that transforms later mutate.
fix: Transforms should return copy.deepcopy(data) with modifications instead of mutating in-place
